5 TypeScript Tricks You Need to Know

By Alex Dev | February 20, 2024 | 6 min read

TypeScript has become the de facto standard for large-scale JavaScript applications. Here are five tricks that will level up your TypeScript game.

1. Const Assertions

Use as const to create deeply readonly literal types:

const config = { api: 'https://api.example.com', timeout: 5000 } as const;

This ensures the object is completely immutable at the type level.

2. Template Literal Types

Create powerful string pattern types:

type EventName = `on${Capitalize<string>}`;

3. Discriminated Unions

The most powerful pattern for type-safe state management:

type Result<T> = { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; error: Error };

Each branch of the union is narrowed by the discriminant property.

4. Branded Types

Prevent accidental value mixing with branded primitives:

type UserId = string & { __brand: 'UserId' };

5. Satisfies Operator

Validate type compatibility without widening:

const palette = { red: [255, 0, 0] } satisfies Record<string, [number, number, number]>;

This is incredibly useful for configuration objects where you want both type checking and literal type inference.

Conclusion

These five patterns cover the most common advanced TypeScript scenarios. Master them and you'll write safer, more expressive code.
